name: Turmeric
overview: Turmeric is a bright yellow-orange spice common in Indian, Southeast Asian, and Middle Eastern cooking. It has also been used as medicine in places like India for centuries to treat issues such as breathing problems.
traditional: A staple in Indian Ayurvedic medicine and Traditional Chinese Medicine for conditions like inflammation, digestive issues, and skin ailments.
active_compounds: Curcumin is the major active component, along with related substances called curcuminoids.
benefits: Potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Research supports its effectiveness in managing inflammatory disorders (e.g., arthritis), metabolic syndrome, anxiety, and muscle soreness.
safety_info: Generally considered safe. Its bioavailability is enhanced when taken with black pepper (bioperine).
origin: Native to South Asia (India and Southeast Asia)
amazon_search_term: organic turmeric curcumin capsules with bioperine

name: Ginkgo Biloba
overview: Ginkgo (Ginkgo biloba) is one of the oldest living tree species and its leaves are among the most extensively studied herbs in use today.
traditional: Widely used in Chinese medicine for thousands of years, and one of the oldest homeopathic plants.
active_compounds: Contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are known for their potent antioxidant effects.
benefits: Known for boosting cognitive health, impacting anxiety, improving memory, and potentially helping with peripheral nerve damage.
safety_info: Ginkgo supplements can interact with blood thinners and may increase the risk of bleeding. Only leaf extract should be used.
origin: Native to China
amazon_search_term: ginkgo biloba extract 120mg capsules

name: Echinacea
overview: Echinacea is a group of flowering plants used as a popular herbal remedy. They're most commonly linked to helping your body fight off infections.
traditional: Used as medicine since Native American times, often as a booster treatment for the common cold and other infections.
active_compounds: Contains polysaccharides, alkamides, and caffeic acid derivatives.
benefits: May help boost the immune system, shorten the duration of colds, and potentially prevent their occurrence.
safety_info: Generally safe for short-term use. People allergic to plants in the daisy family may have an allergic reaction.
origin: Native to North America (Great Plains region)
amazon_search_term: echinacea immune support capsules

name: Chamomile
overview: Chamomile is an herb that comes from the daisy-like flowers of the Asteraceae plant family. It has been consumed for centuries as a natural remedy for several health conditions.
traditional: Noted as a medicinal tool in ancient Egyptian, Greek, and Roman practices, commonly prepared as a tea for calming properties and digestive aid.
active_compounds: Apigenin is a key bioactive compound that interacts with GABA receptors to promote relaxation.
benefits: Promoted as a remedy for anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating gastrointestinal dysfunction.
safety_info: Considered safe by the FDA. May cause increased tiredness if taken with other medications.
origin: Native to Europe and Western Asia
amazon_search_term: organic chamomile tea capsules sleep support

name: Peppermint
overview: Peppermint is a natural hybrid cross between watermint and spearmint, widely recognized for its cooling sensation.
traditional: Used in ancient Egyptian, Greek, and Roman medicine for various digestive and respiratory ailments.
active_compounds: Menthol (up to 48%) and menthone are the primary active volatile oils.
benefits: Significant evidence supports its use for Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS), tension headaches, and soothing digestive discomfort.
safety_info: May worsen symptoms of GERD (Acid Reflux) by relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter. Use with caution during pregnancy.
origin: Hybrid origin; cultivated widely in Europe and North America
amazon_search_term: peppermint oil enteric coated capsules IBS

name: Ginger
overview: Ginger is a flowering plant whose rhizome (ginger root) is a common spice and traditional remedy across Asia and Africa.
traditional: A staple of Ayurvedic and TCM practices for over 2,500 years, primarily for digestive upset, colds, and arthritis.
active_compounds: Rich in gingerols, shogaols, and zingerone, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
benefits: Highly effective for nausea (morning sickness, chemotherapy-induced), pain relief (osteoarthritis, dysmenorrhea), and digestive support.
safety_info: Generally safe. Limit intake to 4g daily. May interact with blood thinners or medications for blood sugar and blood pressure. Safe for pregnancy nausea in moderate amounts (<1.5g/day).
origin: Native to Southeast Asia (likely India or China)
amazon_search_term: organic ginger root capsules nausea relief

name: Lavender
overview: Lavender is an aromatic evergreen shrub widely cultivated for its flowers and essential oils.
traditional: Used for over 2,500 years for its fragrance and therapeutic properties, from ancient mummification to Roman bathing rituals.
active_compounds: Linalool and linalyl acetate are the primary terpene alcohols responsible for its sedative effects.
benefits: Highly effective for reducing anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating mild pain through aromatherapy and oral supplementation.
safety_info: Generally safe. Oral lavender oil can cause minor digestive upset. Topical use is safe for most, but skin irritation is possible.
origin: Native to the Mediterranean region
amazon_search_term: lavender essential oil sleep anxiety supplement

name: Ashwagandha
overview: Ashwagandha, also known as Indian ginseng or winter cherry, is an evergreen shrub in the Solanaceae family. It is a cornerstone of Ayurvedic medicine, classified as a Rasayana herb for promoting health and longevity.
traditional: A key Rasayana herb in Ayurveda for over 3,000 years, traditionally used for rejuvenation, vitality, longevity, and to calm the nerves (Vataroga). The name 'somnifera' reflects its traditional use for sleep.
active_compounds: Steroidal lactones known as withanolides (withaferin A, withanolide A), alkaloids (withanine, somniferine), saponins, and flavonoids.
benefits: Proven to reduce stress, anxiety, and cortisol levels. Research also supports improvements in sleep quality, cognitive function, physical performance, and male fertility/sperm quality.
safety_info: Generally safe for up to 3 months. Avoid during pregnancy (potential abortifacient). May interact with thyroid (increases hormones), blood pressure, diabetes, and sedative medications. Use caution with liver disease or stomach ulcers. Stop 2 weeks before surgery.
origin: Native to India, North Africa, and the Mediterranean
amazon_search_term: ashwagandha KSM-66 stress relief capsules

name: Valerian Root
overview: Valerian is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. In the United States, it is widely used as a dietary supplement for sleep disorders and anxiety.
traditional: Used since ancient Greek and Roman times to treat insomnia, migraines, fatigue, and stomach cramps.
active_compounds: Contains valerenic acid, valepotriates, and volatile oils that are thought to interact with GABA receptors.
benefits: Promoted for improving sleep quality, reducing the time it takes to fall asleep, and managing mild anxiety.
safety_info: Generally safe for short-term use. Avoid prior to surgery or combining with other sedatives/alcohol.
origin: Native to Europe and Asia
amazon_search_term: valerian root sleep supplement capsules

name: St. John's Wort
overview: St. John's Wort is a flowering plant with a long history of use in traditional medicine for mental health conditions.
traditional: Historically used for anxiety, depression, and wound healing since ancient Greek times.
active_compounds: Hypericin and hyperforin are the primary active compounds responsible for its medicinal activity.
benefits: Extensively studied for mild-to-moderate depression; may also support menopausal symptoms and PMS.
safety_info: High potential for drug interactions. Can reduce the effectiveness of birth control and blood thinners. Consult a doctor before use.
origin: Native to Europe and Western Asia
amazon_search_term: St Johns Wort mood support supplement

name: Ginseng
overview: Ginseng refers to species in the Panax genus, primarily Asian (Panax ginseng) and American (Panax quinquefolius). The name Panax comes from the Greek word for 'all-healing'.
traditional: Highly prized in TCM (Traditional Chinese Medicine) as a 'Qi tonic' for energy and vitality. Used for over 2,000 years to improve physical function, immune health, and as an adaptogen.
active_compounds: Contains over 100 ginsenosides (steroidal saponins like Rb1, Rg1), gintonin, polysaccharides (ginsenans), and polyacetylenes.
benefits: Studies support benefits for cognitive function, mental clarity, fatigue reduction, immune modulation, and erectile dysfunction. May also help regulate blood sugar.
safety_info: Safe for short-term use (up to 6 months). Can interact with blood thinners (Warfarin), MAOIs, and diabetes medications. Avoid in hormone-sensitive conditions (estrogen-like effects), pregnancy, or autoimmune disorders. May cause insomnia or headaches.
origin: Asian ginseng native to Korea and China; American ginseng native to eastern North America
amazon_search_term: Korean red ginseng energy vitality capsules

name: Elderberry
overview: Elderberry is the dark purple berry of the European elder tree (Sambucus nigra). It has been used in folk medicine for centuries and is now one of the most popular herbal supplements for immune support.
traditional: Used in European folk medicine for centuries to treat colds, flu, and fever. Native Americans also used elder plants for infections and skin conditions.
active_compounds: Rich in anthocyanins, flavonoids (quercetin, rutin), and vitamins A and C, which contribute to its antioxidant and antiviral properties.
benefits: May reduce the duration and severity of colds and flu. Supports immune function and provides antioxidant protection.
safety_info: Raw or unripe elderberries are toxic and must not be consumed. Cooked or processed forms are safe. May interact with immunosuppressants and diuretics.
origin: Native to Europe and North America
amazon_search_term: elderberry syrup immune support supplement

name: Garlic
overview: Garlic is a plant in the Onion genus, Allium, known for its strong aroma and numerous medicinal properties worldwide.
traditional: Documented use for 5,000 years across Egyptian, Babylonian, Greek, Roman, and Chinese civilizations for strength, infection, and circulation.
active_compounds: Allicin (formed when crushed), alliin, and diallyl disulfide are the primary organosulfur compounds.
benefits: Supports heart health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Possesses antimicrobial and immune-enhancing properties.
safety_info: Safe for most. May cause bad breath and body odor. High medicinal doses can increase the risk of bleeding; use caution with anticoagulants (e.g., Warfarin, Aspirin). Stop intake 2 weeks before surgery.
origin: Native to Central Asia (likely Kyrgyzstan or Tajikistan)
amazon_search_term: odorless garlic supplement cardiovascular health

name: Rosemary
overview: Rosemary is a fragrant evergreen herb native to the Mediterranean region, widely used in cooking and traditional medicine. It has been studied for its potential cognitive and circulatory benefits.
traditional: Used since ancient Greek and Roman times to improve memory, stimulate circulation, and as a culinary herb. Sprigs were placed on graves and worn at weddings as a symbol of remembrance.
active_compounds: Rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and 1,8-cineole are the primary bioactive compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
benefits: May improve memory and concentration, support circulation, and provide antioxidant protection. Aromatherapy with rosemary oil has shown cognitive benefits in studies.
safety_info: Safe in culinary amounts. High doses or essential oil ingestion can cause seizures. Avoid medicinal doses during pregnancy. May interact with blood thinners and ACE inhibitors.
origin: Native to the Mediterranean region
amazon_search_term: rosemary extract memory cognitive supplement

name: Cinnamon
overview: Cinnamon is a spice obtained from the inner bark of trees in the Cinnamomum genus. Ceylon cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum) is considered 'true cinnamon' and is prized for its delicate flavor and lower coumarin content.
traditional: Used for over 4,000 years in ancient Egypt, China, and India for digestive ailments, respiratory conditions, and as a preservative.
active_compounds: Cinnamaldehyde, cinnamic acid, and cinnamate are the primary active compounds. Ceylon cinnamon has lower coumarin levels than Cassia cinnamon.
benefits: May help lower blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity. Possesses antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
safety_info: Ceylon cinnamon is generally safe. Cassia cinnamon contains high coumarin levels that can cause liver damage in large doses. Avoid high doses during pregnancy.
origin: Native to Sri Lanka (Ceylon cinnamon) and southern China (Cassia cinnamon)
amazon_search_term: Ceylon cinnamon blood sugar supplement capsules

name: Sage
overview: Sage is a perennial, evergreen subshrub with woody stems, grayish leaves, and blue to purplish flowers. It is a member of the mint family Lamiaceae and native to the Mediterranean region.
traditional: Used since ancient Greek and Roman times for its medicinal properties, including improving memory, treating sore throats, and as a digestive aid. The name Salvia comes from the Latin 'salvere' meaning 'to save'.
active_compounds: Contains rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, thujone, and camphor. Rich in vitamins K, A, and C.
benefits: Research supports benefits for cognitive function and memory, menopausal hot flashes, blood sugar regulation, and oral health (antimicrobial properties).
safety_info: Safe in culinary amounts. High doses containing thujone can be toxic. Avoid medicinal doses during pregnancy and breastfeeding. May interact with diabetes and sedative medications.
origin: Native to the Mediterranean region
amazon_search_term: sage extract menopause cognitive support supplement

name: Basil
overview: Basil is a culinary herb of the family Lamiaceae (mints). It is a tender plant used in cuisines worldwide, particularly in Italian and Southeast Asian cooking. Holy basil (Tulsi) is a related species with stronger medicinal use.
traditional: Revered as a sacred plant in Hindu tradition (Tulsi/Holy Basil). Used in Ayurvedic medicine for thousands of years as an adaptogen for stress, infections, and inflammation.
active_compounds: Contains eugenol, linalool, rosmarinic acid, and various flavonoids with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
benefits: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Holy basil (Tulsi) has adaptogenic effects for stress reduction. May support blood sugar regulation and cardiovascular health.
safety_info: Generally safe in culinary amounts. High doses of basil essential oil may be toxic. Avoid medicinal doses during pregnancy. May interact with blood thinners.
origin: Native to tropical regions of Central Africa and Southeast Asia
amazon_search_term: holy basil tulsi adaptogen stress supplement

name: Fennel
overview: Fennel is a Mediterranean-originating herb characterized by its licorice-like flavor and feathery leaves. It is a key ingredient in many culinary dishes and traditional remedies.
traditional: Used since ancient Egyptian times for digestive relief and in Greek medicine to support lactation and regulate menstruation.
active_compounds: Rich in anethole, fenchone, and estragole, which contribute to its carminative effects.
benefits: Effective for treating gastrointestinal distress, infantile colic, and potentially easing menstrual cramps (dysmenorrhea).
safety_info: Generally safe in food amounts. High doses or essential oil use should be avoided during pregnancy due to estrogenic activity. May interact with certain antibiotics like Ciprofloxacin.
origin: Native to the Mediterranean region
amazon_search_term: fennel seed digestive supplement capsules

name: Dandelion
overview: Dandelion is a flowering plant widely considered a weed but valued in herbal medicine for its nutritional and medicinal properties. Both the root and leaves are used therapeutically.
traditional: Used in traditional Chinese medicine and European folk medicine for liver and gallbladder conditions, digestive issues, and as a diuretic.
active_compounds: Contains taraxacin, taraxacerin, inulin, sesquiterpene lactones, and high levels of vitamins A, C, and K.
benefits: Acts as a natural diuretic, supports liver health, aids digestion, and provides significant nutritional value. May help regulate blood sugar and cholesterol.
safety_info: Generally safe. May cause allergic reactions in people sensitive to ragweed or related plants. Avoid with bile duct obstruction or gallstones. May interact with diuretics and lithium.
origin: Native to Eurasia; now widespread worldwide
amazon_search_term: dandelion root liver detox supplement

name: Lemon Balm
overview: Lemon Balm is a lemon-scented herb from the mint family, native to Europe and North Africa, commonly used in teas and essential oils.
traditional: Used since the Middle Ages to reduce stress and anxiety, promote sleep, and soothe digestive issues.
active_compounds: Contains high levels of rosmarinic acid, citral, and citronellal.
benefits: Calms the nervous system, improves cognitive function and mood, and has antiviral properties (effective topically for cold sores).
safety_info: Generally safe for short-term use. May cause drowsiness; use caution with other sedatives. May interfere with thyroid medications; those with hypothyroidism should consult a doctor.
origin: Native to Europe and North Africa
amazon_search_term: lemon balm extract anxiety sleep supplement

name: Licorice Root
overview: Licorice root is the root of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant, valued for its sweetness and therapeutic applications in diverse global traditions.
traditional: Used widely in TCM, Ayurveda, and ancient Greek medicine for respiratory congestion, digestive ulcers, and liver support.
active_compounds: Contains glycyrrhizin, glycyrrhetinic acid, and various flavonoids and liquiritin.
benefits: Soothes the digestive tract (GERD, ulcers), helps relieve sore throats and coughs, and possesses anti-inflammatory properties.
safety_info: Chronic use of non-DGL (deglycyrrhizinated) licorice can lead to potassium loss, high blood pressure, and heart issues. Avoid during pregnancy and with diuretics or heart medications. Deglycyrrhizinated (DGL) forms are safer for continuous use.
origin: Native to the Mediterranean region and southwestern Asia
amazon_search_term: DGL licorice root digestive supplement

name: Calendula
overview: Calendula (pot marigold) is a flowering plant in the daisy family, widely used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, particularly in topical applications.
traditional: Used in traditional European and Ayurvedic medicine for centuries to treat skin wounds, burns, rashes, and infections. Also used internally for digestive and menstrual complaints.
active_compounds: Contains triterpenoids (oleanolic acid glycosides), flavonoids (quercetin, isorhamnetin), carotenoids, and polysaccharides.
benefits: Highly effective for wound healing, reducing skin inflammation, and treating dermatitis and eczema. May also have antifungal and antimicrobial properties.
safety_info: Generally safe for topical use. May cause allergic reactions in people sensitive to ragweed or other Asteraceae plants. Avoid during pregnancy (may stimulate uterine contractions). Limited safety data for internal use.
origin: Native to the Mediterranean region and southwestern Asia
amazon_search_term: calendula cream skin healing wound care
